ddls:
CREATE TABLE "patients" (
  "patient_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each patient */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the patient */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the patient */,
  "dob" TEXT /* Date of birth of the patient */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the patient */,
  "contact_number" TEXT /* Contact number of the patient */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the patient */,
  "insurance_provider" TEXT /* Insurance provider of the patient */,
  "policy_number" TEXT /* Policy number of the patient's insurance */,
  "expiration_date" TEXT /* Expiration date of the patient's insurance policy */,
  "emergency_contact_name" TEXT /* Name of the patient's emergency contact */,
  "emergency_contact_number" TEXT /* Contact number of the patient's emergency contact */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("patient_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "medical_conditions" (
  "condition_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each medical condition */,
  "patient_id" INTEGER /* ID of the patient with this condition */,
  "condition_name" TEXT /* Name of the medical condition */,
  "diagnosis_date" TEXT /* Date when the condition was diagnosed */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the condition (e.g., active, resolved) */,
  "condition_description" TEXT /* Description of the medical condition */,
  "severity_level" TEXT /* Severity level of the condition (e.g., mild, moderate, severe) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("condition_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_medical_conditions_patient_id FOREIGN KEY ("patient_id") REFERENCES patients ("patient_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "medications" (
  "medication_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each medication */,
  "condition_id" INTEGER /* ID of the medical condition this medication is associated with */,
  "medication_name" TEXT /* Name of the medication */,
  "dosage" TEXT /* Dosage of the medication */,
  "prescription_date" TEXT /* Date when the medication was prescribed */,
  "last_change_date" TEXT /* Date of the last change made to the medication */,
  "frequency" TEXT /* Frequency of the medication (e.g., daily, weekly) */,
  "duration" TEXT /* Duration of the medication */,
  "side_effects" TEXT /* Potential side effects of the medication */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("medication_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_medications_condition_id FOREIGN KEY ("condition_id") REFERENCES medical_conditions ("condition_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "prescriptions" (
  "prescription_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each prescription */,
  "medication_id" INTEGER /* ID of the medication being prescribed */,
  "patient_id" INTEGER /* ID of the patient receiving the prescription */,
  "doctor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the doctor who prescribed the medication */,
  "prescription_date" TEXT /* Date when the prescription was made */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the prescription (e.g., active, completed, canceled) */,
  "refills" INTEGER /* Number of refills allowed */,
  "refill_expiration_date" TEXT /* Date when the refills expire */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("prescription_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_prescriptions_doctor_id FOREIGN KEY ("doctor_id") REFERENCES doctors ("doctor_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_prescriptions_patient_id FOREIGN KEY ("patient_id") REFERENCES patients ("patient_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_prescriptions_medication_id FOREIGN KEY ("medication_id") REFERENCES medications ("medication_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "doctors" (
  "doctor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each doctor */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the doctor */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the doctor */,
  "specialization" TEXT /* Specialization of the doctor */,
  "contact_number" TEXT /* Contact number of the doctor */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the doctor */,
  "specialization_area" TEXT /* Area of specialization (e.g., cardiology, oncology) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("doctor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "pharmacists" (
  "pharmacist_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each pharmacist */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the pharmacist */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the pharmacist */,
  "license_number" TEXT /* License number of the pharmacist */,
  "contact_number" TEXT /* Contact number of the pharmacist */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the pharmacist */,
  "specialization" TEXT /* Area of specialization (e.g., clinical pharmacy, community pharmacy) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("pharmacist_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "appointments" (
  "appointment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each appointment */,
  "patient_id" INTEGER /* ID of the patient for the appointment */,
  "doctor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the doctor for the appointment */,
  "appointment_date" TEXT /* Date of the appointment */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the appointment (e.g., scheduled, completed, canceled) */,
  "appointment_type" TEXT /* Type of appointment (e.g., follow-up, consultation, surgery) */,
  "appointment_notes" TEXT /* Notes about the appointment */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("appointment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "lab_tests" (
  "test_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each lab test */,
  "patient_id" INTEGER /* ID of the patient who underwent the test */,
  "test_name" TEXT /* Name of the lab test performed */,
  "test_date" TEXT /* Date the test was conducted */,
  "results" TEXT /* Results of the lab test */,
  "test_type" TEXT /* Type of lab test (e.g., blood test, imaging test) */,
  "test_results_status" TEXT /* Status of the test results (e.g., pending, completed) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("test_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_lab_tests_patient_id FOREIGN KEY ("patient_id") REFERENCES patients ("patient_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "medication_changes" (
  "change_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each medication change */,
  "medication_id" INTEGER /* ID of the medication that was changed */,
  "change_type" TEXT /* Type of change (e.g., added, modified, discontinued) */,
  "change_date" TEXT /* Date the change was made */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Notes regarding the change */,
  "change_reason" TEXT /* Reason for the medication change */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("change_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_medication_changes_medication_id FOREIGN KEY ("medication_id") REFERENCES medications ("medication_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "allergies" (
  "allergy_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each allergy */,
  "patient_id" INTEGER /* ID of the patient with the allergy */,
  "allergy_name" TEXT /* Name of the allergen causing the allergy */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity of the allergy (e.g., mild, severe) */,
  "reaction" TEXT /* Description of the reaction to the allergen */,
  "allergy_type" TEXT /* Type of allergy (e.g., food, medicine, environmental) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("allergy_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_allergies_patient_id FOREIGN KEY ("patient_id") REFERENCES patients ("patient_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role (e.g., doctor, nurse, pharmacist) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  "role_permissions" TEXT /* Permissions assigned to the role (e.g., view patient records, edit prescriptions) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the user */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role assigned to the user */,
  "contact_number" TEXT /* Contact number of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES user_roles ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the record */,
  "patient_id" INTEGER /* ID of the patient whose record is accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the access occurred */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "access_details" TEXT /* Details of the access (e.g., specific data accessed, actions taken) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_patient_id FOREIGN KEY ("patient_id") REFERENCES patients ("patient_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "medication_administration" (
  "administration_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each medication administration */,
  "medication_id" INTEGER /* ID of the medication being administered */,
  "patient_id" INTEGER /* ID of the patient receiving the medication */,
  "administration_date" TEXT /* Date when the medication was administered */,
  "dose_given" TEXT /* Dose of the medication given */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("administration_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_medication_administration_patient_id FOREIGN KEY ("patient_id") REFERENCES patients ("patient_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_medication_administration_medication_id FOREIGN KEY ("medication_id") REFERENCES medications ("medication_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "immunizations" (
  "immunization_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each immunization */,
  "patient_id" INTEGER /* ID of the patient who received the immunization */,
  "vaccine_name" TEXT /* Name of the vaccine administered */,
  "immunization_date" TEXT /* Date when the immunization was given */,
  "dosage" TEXT /* Dosage of the vaccine administered */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("immunization_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_immunizations_patient_id FOREIGN KEY ("patient_id") REFERENCES patients ("patient_id")
)
